title: IIOS Signed Capability Envelope
description: A Capability Payload plus its cryptographic proof of issuance. See docs/23_CAPABILITY_MODEL.md. A valid envelope proves Governance issued this payload unmodified; it does NOT by itself prove the capability is currently usable — consumption state (revocation, uses-remaining, nonce reuse) is tracked server-side, keyed by capability_id, and is never part of this signed structure.

algorithm.description: Signature algorithm identifier, e.g. 'Ed25519'. Concrete choice (JWS, PASETO, or a bespoke scheme) is an open question for the Owner and security review; see docs/23_CAPABILITY_MODEL.md Open questions.
algorithm.$comment: This schema fixes only that an algorithm identifier is present. It does not select, and must not be read as endorsing, a specific cryptographic format.

key_id.description: Identifies which signing key was used, for rotation and verification key lookup.

signature.description: Signature over the canonical serialization of `payload` only. Base64url-encoded or algorithm-appropriate encoding; concrete encoding is fixed once the algorithm decision (Open questions) is made.

capabilityPayload.title: IIOS Capability Payload
capabilityPayload.description: The signed claims. Immutable once issued. Consumption state (uses_remaining, revoked) is deliberately NOT part of this object — see docs/23_CAPABILITY_MODEL.md 'What is NOT in the payload, and why'.

issuer.description: Identifies the Governance API instance/environment that issued this payload.

subject.description: Bound actor. Not transferable to another actor.

audience.description: The executor/service this capability is valid for. Prevents a capability issued for one execution surface being replayed against a different one.

resource.description: Concrete resource_ref. Exact match required at consumption.

policy_version.description: Policy this payload was evaluated and issued under; checked against the currently active policy_version at consumption, not only at issuance.

approval_id.description: Required (non-null) for any capability derived from a Class C action item.

not_before.description: Capability is not valid before this instant.

expires_at.description: MUST be short-lived and close to issued_at. Exact ceiling per class is an Owner-configured policy parameter, not hardcoded here.

max_uses.description: Intended use ceiling declared at issuance. The LIVE remaining-use count is server-side state keyed by capability_id, not a field in this payload — see docs/23_CAPABILITY_MODEL.md.

constraints.description: Additional scope-narrowing conditions the executor must satisfy, e.g. max amount, allowed commit SHA.

nonce.description: Unique per issuance. Combined with capability_id, detects replay of an already-consumed capability independent of the server-side use counter.
